display.purpose: Page titles and workspace entity names.

body.purpose: Evidence summaries, product copy, and readable explanation.

label.purpose: Truth metadata, status terms, and compact descriptors.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Fast hover, focus, and selected-state transitions.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Reveal or drilldown state changes that need more clarity.

components.name: Sidebar Navigation Link
components.description: Primary navigation item inside the slate console shell.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { display: block; background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 7px; color: oklch(0.78 0.025 250); font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-weight: 650; padding: 9px 10px; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { background: oklch(0.32 0.045 250); border-color: oklch(0.44 0.04 250); color: oklch(0.98 0.006 78); outline: 2px solid color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.62 0.15 58), transparent 55%); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Command-search field for repositories, services, and workloads.
components.css: .ds-search { display: grid; gap: 8px; color: oklch(0.46 0.018 250); font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 0.88rem; font-weight: 650; } .ds-search input { background: oklch(0.998 0.003 78); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.018 78); border-radius: 7px; color: oklch(0.25 0.018 250); min-height: 42px; padding: 0 12px; } .ds-search input:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.62 0.15 58); outline: 2px solid color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.62 0.15 58), transparent 55%); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Product Panel
components.description: Flat tonal container for runtime summaries, ledgers, evidence, and catalog sections.
components.html:
Runtime summary

896 repositories indexed, zero queue items outstanding.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: oklch(0.985 0.006 78); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.018 78); border-radius: 8px; color: oklch(0.25 0.018 250); font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; padding: 16px; } .ds-panel h2 { font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.25; margin: 0 0 14px; } .ds-panel p { color: oklch(0.46 0.018 250); line-height: 1.55; margin: 0; max-width: 65ch; }

components.name: Ledger Row Button
components.description: Compact interactive row for queue ledger, graph details, and evidence inspection.
components.css: .ds-row-button { background: oklch(0.998 0.003 78); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.018 78); border-radius: 7px; color: oklch(0.25 0.018 250); cursor: pointer; display: grid; gap: 5px; font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; padding: 11px 12px; text-align: left; width: 100%; } .ds-row-button strong { font-size: 1.2rem; } .ds-row-button:hover { background: oklch(0.965 0.012 78); } .ds-row-button:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.62 0.15 58); outline: 2px solid color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.62 0.15 58), transparent 55%); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Relationship Graph Canvas
components.description: Readable graph field for deployment and relationship evidence.
components.css: .ds-graph { background: oklch(0.998 0.003 78); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.018 78); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; } .ds-graph svg { display: block; width: 100%; } .ds-graph-edge { fill: none; stroke: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.52 0.14 260), oklch(0.82 0.018 78) 22%); stroke-linecap: round; stroke-width: 2.4; } .ds-graph-node { fill: oklch(0.985 0.006 78); stroke: oklch(0.82 0.018 78); stroke-width: 1.5; } .ds-graph-node-relationship { fill: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.52 0.14 260), oklch(0.985 0.006 78) 88%); stroke: oklch(0.52 0.14 260); } .ds-graph text { fill: oklch(0.25 0.018 250); font: 700 14px ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; }

components.name: Evidence Row
components.description: Two-column evidence row that keeps summary and proof metadata together.
components.html:
Source
helm-charts
Path
values.yaml
components.css: .ds-evidence-row { align-items: start; background: oklch(0.998 0.003 78); border: 1px solid oklch(0.82 0.018 78); border-radius: 7px; color: oklch(0.25 0.018 250); display: grid; gap: 18px; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(220px, 0.55fr); padding: 14px; } .ds-evidence-row button { background: transparent; border: 0; color: inherit; cursor: pointer; padding: 0; text-align: left; } .ds-evidence-row p { color: oklch(0.46 0.018 250); line-height: 1.45; margin: 5px 0 0; } .ds-evidence-row dl { display: grid; gap: 8px; margin: 0; } .ds-evidence-row dt { color: oklch(0.56 0.018 250); font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 650; } .ds-evidence-row dd { font-weight: 700; margin: 0; } .ds-evidence-row:focus-within { border-color: oklch(0.52 0.14 260); }

narrative.overview:
Eshu Console is a bright operating room for graph truth. It should feel like a living map room: warm enough for non-engineers to enter, precise enough for operators to trust, and vibrant enough that relationships, freshness, and evidence are visually alive.

The system rejects generic SaaS dashboard patterns, dark-card sameness, and decorative data theater. The console is not a homepage and not a database viewer. It is a working product surface where the story comes first, then the proof is immediately available.
keyCharacteristics: Warm light canvas with cool technical structure.
Dense, scannable product UI with restrained but meaningful color.
Evidence-first surfaces that separate known, missing, inferred, and stale.
Readable graph labels and table alternatives for broad organizational use.
Flat tonal layering instead of decorative shadows or glass effects.

rules.name: The Vibrancy Serves Truth Rule
rules.body: Color is for relationship type, status, freshness, risk, and selection. Color is never filler.

rules.name: The No Dark Default Rule
rules.body: Do not make the console dark by default. The product must feel bright, readable, and usable across the whole organization.

rules.name: The Label Legibility Rule
rules.body: Graph labels, relationship verbs, and table headers must stay readable before decoration is considered.

rules.name: The Plain Language First Rule
rules.body: Use plain-language summaries before exposing technical identifiers, but keep the identifiers available for drilldown.

rules.name: The Tonal Layer Rule
rules.body: Use surface-paper, surface-raised, and field-porcelain to create hierarchy before introducing any shadow.

rules.name: The No Glass Rule
rules.body: Glassmorphism, decorative blur, and translucent panels are forbidden unless a future interaction needs a true overlay.

dos: Do keep the console light, vibrant, and readable by default.
Do use relationship-blue for graph relationships and selected evidence.
Do use accent-amber for focus and rare high-value emphasis.
Do show story and proof together: summary, graph, table, and drilldown.
Do label status, freshness, truth, and missing evidence in text as well as color.
Do keep charts and graphs readable for executives, support, finance, and engineers.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS dashboard patterns with oversized metric cards and decorative gradients.
Don't use dark-card sameness where every panel looks equally important.
Don't ship unreadable relationship graphs with clipped names or unlabeled edges.
Don't create key/value evidence dumps that require users to reverse-engineer the story.
Don't use mock data or demo phrasing on real-data surfaces.
Don't use engineer-only terminology without plain-language context.
Don't add decorative UI that makes status, risk, freshness, or evidence harder to read.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism, nested cards, or modal-first drilldowns.
